Question ID: architecture-derived-B5-combinatorial-probability-random-chords
Title: B5. Combinatorial Probability (Random Chords)

Question:
Disk divided into 4 quadrants.
Add 25 random chords with endpoints in different quadrants.
What is the expected number of regions?

Grading target:
Exact numerical match.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

Node started: Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: architecture-derived-B5-combinatorial-probability-random-chords
Title: B5. Combinatorial Probability (Random Chords)

Question:
Disk divided into 4 quadrants.
Add 25 random chords with endpoints in different quadrants.
What is the expected number of regions?

Grading target:
Exact numerical match.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

Node started: Consider a disk divided into 4 equal quadrants. You draw 25 chords, each chord having its two endpoints located in two different quadrants (no chord has both endpoints in the same quadrant). Assuming chords are placed randomly and independently under these constraints, determine the expected number of regions formed inside the disk by these chords. Provide your answer as an exact numerical value. Format your response exactly as follows:

FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification explaining your reasoning and any formulas used>
